title: cynometra commersoniana
intro: Cynometra commersoniana, also known as the Java or Burmese almond, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. Its health benefits include treating fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant's seeds, bark, and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts that can be used to treat various health ailments.
